questionText: The time, $X$ minutes, that customers wait to be served is modelled by $X \sim N(40, 10^2)$. Find $P(X > 55)$, giving your answer to $4$ significant figures.

steps.description: State the distribution and what is required
steps.explanation: The waiting time is normal with mean $\mu = 40$ minutes and standard deviation $\sigma = 10$ minutes. We want the probability that a customer waits longer than $55$ minutes.
We convert the waiting time into a $z$-score by subtracting the mean and dividing by the standard deviation, so that we can use the single standard normal distribution.
The time $55$ is $15$ minutes above the mean, which is $1.5$ standard deviations of $10$. So $55$ minutes corresponds to $z = 1.5$.
The calculator gives the area to the left, $\Phi$, but we want the area to the right. Since the total area is $1$, the right-hand tail is $1$ minus the left-hand area.
The lower-tail value at $z = 1.5$ is $\Phi(1.5) = 0.93319$, so subtracting from $1$ leaves the upper tail as $0.06681$ to $4$ significant figures.
So only about $6.7\%$ of customers wait longer than $55$ minutes. The probability is small, which fits the fact that $55$ minutes is well above the average wait.
